_comment: Electronegativity suppressions for TermWatch. Documents known-acceptable security findings.

CSP_GLOBAL_CHECK.reason: App view loads only local file:// HTML. Video view loads untrusted content but has contextIsolation=true and nodeIntegration=false. CSP for video view is managed by the target sites themselves.

LIMIT_NAVIGATION_GLOBAL_CHECK.reason: Video view intentionally navigates to user-provided URLs (streaming sites). Navigation is validated in ipc-handlers.js (https-only, max 2048 chars). App view loads only file:// URLs and does not navigate.

PERMISSION_REQUEST_HANDLER_GLOBAL_CHECK.reason: Video view partition 'persist:video' uses default permissions. DRM content requires media key system access. A permission handler could be added in a future phase to restrict non-essential permissions.

known_architectural_decisions.nodeIntegration_appView: App view uses nodeIntegration=true because xterm.js Terminal class constructors cannot pass through Electron's contextBridge (structured clone fails on class instances).
known_architectural_decisions.contextIsolation_appView: App view uses contextIsolation=false for the same xterm.js reason. This view loads only local file:// HTML, never untrusted content.
known_architectural_decisions.sandbox_disabled: Sandbox is disabled because node-pty requires Node.js native module access to spawn pseudo-terminal processes.

parsing_note: Per-file atomic checks may fail with 'Invalid Version' because TermWatch uses the CastLabs Electron fork (git URL in package.json, not a semver-parseable version). Global checks run successfully.
